U
Oracle Events
- Retrieve the loan record using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number = LN-2026-00009 to extract the loan_id (los_loan_ad53e691489a) for downstream operations.
- Retrieve underwriting conditions using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id = los_loan_ad53e691489a to identify all outstanding prior-to-doc and prior-to-closing conditions blocking the file.
- Retrieve the document checklist using mortgage_los_get_documents with loan_id = los_loan_ad53e691489a to inspect document checklist status and identify missing, stale, or inconsistent items.
- Verify document existence in the filesystem using filesystem_show_data (with offset/limit as needed) and/or filesystem_get_file_info with known paths to confirm whether supporting artifacts exist outside the LOS checklist narrative.
- Search internal email communications using search_emails with queries such as "Darren Singh" and "LN-2026-00009" to identify communications related to loan issues, closing readiness, or missing documentation.
- Search Slack discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries such as "Darren Singh" and "LN-2026-00009" to identify internal discussions, flags, or inconsistencies related to the file.
- Resolve borrower and contact context using crm_search_contacts or contacts_search_contacts with "Darren Singh" to confirm borrower identity and retrieve contact context if needed for escalation or communication.
- Update the loan record using mortgage_los_update_loan with loan_id = los_loan_ad53e691489a and notes documenting that the loan must not proceed to closing on 2026-04-12 until outstanding conditions and disclosure gaps are resolved (use only status values accepted by the LOS; universe loans use statuses such as processing, conditional_approval, clear_to_close, closed, denied; avoid inventing new status strings).
- Update condition records using mortgage_los_update_condition for los_cond_d7fdad61f9fa, los_cond_74d7f24385f5, and los_cond_739ecac87a02 to add notes documenting stale or unresolved requirements while keeping status outstanding where appropriate.
- Log activity in the LOS using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id = los_loan_ad53e691489a to record a structured summary of findings, including the April 12 closing risk, outstanding conditions, blank LE/CD dates on the loan record, and file readiness concerns.
- Notify internal stakeholders via Slack using conversations_add_message with channel_id = C002 (#loan-processing) or C003 (#closings) and a payload that references LN-2026-00009, states the file is not ready to close, names Carlos Rivera and Elena Marchetti (or carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com and elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com), and summarizes key blockers.
- Send an internal email escalation using send_email with sender = tyler.washington@keystonemortgage.com, recipients including carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com and elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com (cc acceptable), subject and content summarizing that LN-2026-00009 is not ready to close on April 12 and listing blocking issues. Do not send to borrower or external parties until internal alignment is complete.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

12 The model must use mortgage_los_get_documents, or retrieve document checklist data via mortgage_los_get_loan (which returns full loan detail including embedded documents), to review the document status for loan los_loan_ad53e691489a / LN-2026-00009.
tool selection

✓Run #2The final response identifies at least 7 specific blockers, including blank LE/CD dates (TRID violation), rate lock expiration, all 3 outstanding conditions (appraisal, pay stubs, bank statements), missing homeowners insurance, unauthorized access, duplicate document folders, and Slack/LOS borrower mismatch.
✗Run #3The agent produced no final text response, so it did not enumerate specific blockers preventing closing. The blocking issues were present in the retrieved data but never communicated in a response.

✓Run #5The final response identifies multiple specific blockers: expired rate lock (4/15), both LE/CD sent dates null (TRID violation), 3 named outstanding conditions (appraisal clearance, pay stub within 30 days, bank statements), and stale income/asset docs — well exceeding the two-blocker threshold.
✓Run #6The final response identifies many specific blockers including: (1) LE never sent (TRID violation), (2) CD never sent (TRID violation), (3) rate lock expired, (4) three outstanding UW conditions (appraisal, pay stub, bank statements), (5) missing/unlinked documents, and (6) data integrity concerns — well exceeding the two-blocker threshold.
